examples.example_context: These templates support Software Developer practice as defined in developer.xml.

template_block.name: summary
template_block.title: Summary
template_block.statement: Add server-side validation to postcode input.

template_block.name: why-this-change-is-needed
template_block.title: Why this change is needed
template_block.statement: Supports the Need Statement:
template_block.quoted_rule: Caseworkers need reliable address data so that downstream checks are accurate.

checklist.entry: Added schema validation for postcode field

examples.example_context: ```html <form novalidate> <div class="govuk-form-group govuk-form-group--error"> <label class="govuk-label" for="postcode"> Postcode </label>
Error: Enter a valid UK postcode
```
it("rejects invalid postcode", () => { expect(() => validatePostcode("12345")) .toThrow("Invalid UK postcode"); }); }); ```
```js import request from "supertest"; import app from "../app";
describe("POST /address", () => { it("returns validation error", async () => { const res = await request(app) .post("/address") .send({ postcode: "xxx" });

template_block.name: security-safe-logging-pattern
template_block.title: Security-Safe Logging Pattern
template_block.statement: ``js logger.info("Address submission failed validation", { field: "postcode", reason: "regex_mismatch" }); ``

template_block.name: context
template_block.title: Context
template_block.statement: Inconsistent postcode formats caused downstream failures.

template_block.name: decision
template_block.title: Decision
template_block.statement: Validate postcodes server-side using a strict schema.

template_block.name: consequences
template_block.title: Consequences
template_block.statement: – Some legacy submissions now rejected

template_block.name: definition-of-done-developer
template_block.title: Definition of Done (Developer)
template_block.statement: A change is done when:

checklist.entry: What user need does this satisfy?
Will this be safe in production at scale?

template_block.name: appendix-gov-uk-prototype-kit-ai-context
template_block.title: Appendix: GOV.UK Prototype Kit AI Context
template_block.statement: The following section is incorporated directly from ai-context.xml. It is authoritative for GOV.UK Prototype Kit development and must be followed whenever working in prototypes.
liquid: false

template_block.name: ai-guidelines-for-gov-uk-prototype-kit
template_block.title: AI guidelines for GOV.UK Prototype Kit
template_block.quoted_rule: This file provides context for AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) working on this prototype. It should be read automatically when the AI accesses the repository.

template_block.name: how-to-use-this-file
template_block.title: How to use this file
template_block.statement: This file helps AI assistants generate correct, accessible, GOV.UK-compliant code for your prototype. Here's how to get the most from it:

template_block.name: automatic-loading
template_block.title: Automatic loading
template_block.statement: Most AI-enabled editors (Cursor, Windsurf, Cline) will automatically detect and use ai-context.xml in your project root. For Claude Projects, add it to the project knowledge. If using GitHub Copilot or Claude Code within an IDE like Visual Studio Code, use the following recommended prompts.

template_block.name: recommended-prompts
template_block.title: Recommended prompts
template_block.statement: When starting work, ask your AI assistant to confirm it has this context:
template_block.quoted_rule: "Have you read the ai-context.xml file? Confirm you understand the GOV.UK Prototype Kit conventions before we start."
template_block.statement: For specific tasks, reference the guidelines directly:
template_block.quoted_rule: "Following the content design principles in ai-context.xml, write the error messages for this form." "Create a new question page for collecting date of birth, using the conventions documented in ai-context.xml." "Review this page against the accessibility checklist in ai-context.xml."

checklist.entry: Be specific about versions – mention which prototype version you're working in (e.g. "in /v3/")
Reference patterns – point to existing pages that do something similar
Ask for constraints first – get the AI to confirm what it can't do with GOV.UK components before generating code
Request macro syntax – always ask for Nunjucks macros, not raw HTML

row.cell: The five non-negotiable rules for this codebase
Directory layout, versioning, and naming conventions
Plain English, tone, capitalisation, and GOV.UK style
Formatting, indentation, and general coding principles
Links to live documentation for components, styles, and patterns

template_block.name: project-context
template_block.title: Project context
template_block.statement: This is a GOV.UK Prototype Kit project using:

checklist.entry: GOV.UK Design System components and patterns
All pages must extend the layout template – use {% extends "layouts/main.html" %}
Use Nunjucks macros – prefer {{ govukButton({...}) }} over raw HTML
Only use existing GOV.UK classes – no custom CSS or inline styles
Accessibility is built-in – components are WCAG 2.2 AA compliant by design
Flag gaps – if something isn't possible with existing components, say so

template_block.name: file-structure
template_block.title: File structure
template_block.statement: ``txt app/ ├── views/ │ ├── layouts/ │ │ └── main.html # Base layout – don't modify unless necessary │ ├── includes/ # Reusable partials │ │ ├── _header.html │ │ └── _footer.html │ ├── index.html # Lists all prototype versions │ ├── v1/ # Version 1 │ │ ├── index.html # Version overview/notes (optional) │ │ ├── start.html │ │ ├── question-1.html │ │ ├── question-2.html │ │ ├── check-answers.html │ │ └── confirmation.html │ ├── v2/ # Version 2 │ │ ├── index.html │ │ ├── start.html │ │ ├── question-1.html │ │ ├── question-1b.html # New page added in v2 │ │ └── ... │ └── v3/ # Current working version │ └── ... ├── assets/ │ ├── sass/ # Custom styles (use sparingly) │ ├── javascript/ # Custom JS │ └── images/ ├── routes.js # Express routes and branching logic └── data/ └── session-data-defaults.js # Default form values ``

checklist.entry: Never delete old versions – keep them for reference and comparison
Copy, don't move – when starting a new version, copy the previous folder
Document changes – update the main index.html with what changed and why
Use descriptive version notes – "Added address lookup" not "Updates"
Keep routes separate – each version should have its own route paths

template_block.name: main-index-page-app-views-index-html
template_block.title: Main index page (app/views/index.html)
template_block.statement: The root index.html should list all prototype versions with metadata showing dates, phase, and research status. Here is a suggested layout:
```nunjucks {% extends "layouts/main.html" %}
{% set pageName = "Prototype versions" %}

{% block content %}

{{ serviceName }}
{{ govukInsetText({ text: "This is a prototype for user research. It is not a real service." }) }}
Prototype versions

Version 3

{{ govukTag({ text: "February 2026", classes: "govuk-tag--grey govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Alpha", classes: "govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Changes from R1", classes: "govuk-tag--green govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Tested in R2", classes: "govuk-tag--yellow" }) }}

Iteration based on recommendations from first round of UR. Tested in a second round of UR.
<hr class="govuk-section-break govuk-section-break--l govuk-section-break--visible">

Version 2

{{ govukTag({ text: "February 2026", classes: "govuk-tag--grey govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Alpha", classes: "govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Tested in R1", classes: "govuk-tag--yellow" }) }}

Iteration based on internal review and crit. Tested in a first round of UR.
<hr class="govuk-section-break govuk-section-break--l govuk-section-break--visible">

Version 1

{{ govukTag({ text: "January 2026", classes: "govuk-tag--grey govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Alpha" }) }}

Initial prototype.
{% endblock %} ```

template_block.name: suggested-tag-conventions-for-version-metadata
template_block.title: Suggested tag conventions for version metadata

row.cell: Recommendations implemented from a research round

template_block.statement: Keep descriptions concise – one line explaining what changed or was tested.

template_block.name: version-specific-index-optional
template_block.title: Version-specific index (optional)
template_block.statement: Each version folder can have its own index.html for version-specific notes. Here is a suggested layout:
```nunjucks {% extends "layouts/main.html" %}
{% set pageName = "Version 2" %}
{% block content %} <div class="govuk-grid-row"> <div class="govuk-grid-column-two-thirds">

{{ serviceName }}

Version 2

{{ govukTag({ text: "February 2026", classes: "govuk-tag--grey govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Alpha", classes: "govuk-!-margin-right-1" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Tested in R1", classes: "govuk-tag--yellow" }) }}
{{ govukButton({ text: "View prototype", href: "/v2/start" }) }}

Changes in this version

  • Added address lookup (postcode search)
  • Split eligibility into two questions
  • Removed "other" option from contact preference

Research notes

Tested with 5 users on 15 March 2024.
{% endblock %} ```

template_block.name: content-design-principles
template_block.title: Content design principles
template_block.statement: GOV.UK content follows strict guidelines. Apply these when writing any user-facing text.

template_block.name: plain-english
template_block.title: Plain English
template_block.statement: Plain English is mandatory. Even specialist or technical content must be clear.

template_block.name: tone-and-voice
template_block.title: Tone and voice

checklist.entry: Write like you're talking to the user one-to-one
Be direct and confident, not formal or stuffy

template_block.statement:
```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Direct, conversational #}

You'll need to provide your passport number.

We'll send you a confirmation email.

{# ✗ Avoid: Formal, impersonal #}

Applicants are required to provide their passport number.

A confirmation email will be dispatched to the user.

```

template_block.name: capitalisation
template_block.title: Capitalisation
template_block.statement: Use sentence case for everything except proper nouns.

```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Sentence case #}

Apply for a driving licence

{{ govukButton({ text: "Continue" }) }}

{# ✗ Avoid: Title case #}

Apply For A Driving Licence

{{ govukButton({ text: "Continue Application" }) }} ```

template_block.name: headings-and-labels
template_block.title: Headings and labels

checklist.entry: Front-load with keywords (put important words first)
Use verbs for actions ("Apply for...", "Check your...")
Questions work well for form pages ("What is your name?")

template_block.statement:
```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Clear, front-loaded #}

What is your date of birth?

Upload your photo

{# ✗ Avoid: Vague, buried keywords #}

Personal information entry form

Documents and supporting evidence

```

checklist.entry: Use verbs that describe the action
Be specific about what happens next

template_block.name: error-messages
template_block.title: Error messages
template_block.statement: Form validation is not usually required in prototypes. However, if it is, error messages must:

checklist.entry: Tell the user how to fix it
Use the same words as the label

template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Specific, actionable #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Email address" }, errorMessage: { text: "Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com" } }) }}
{{ govukDateInput({ errorMessage: { text: "Date of birth must include a year" } }) }}
{# ✗ Avoid: Vague, unhelpful #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Email address" }, errorMessage: { text: "Invalid input" } }) }}
{{ govukDateInput({ errorMessage: { text: "Error: validation failed" } }) }} ```

template_block.name: hint-text
template_block.title: Hint text

checklist.entry: Explain what to enter, not why you're asking
Give examples in the format you expect

template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Helpful, specific #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "National Insurance number" }, hint: { text: "It's on your National Insurance card. For example, 'QQ 12 34 56 C'." } }) }}

{# ✗ Avoid: Unnecessary explanation #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "National Insurance number" }, hint: { text: "We need this to verify your identity and check your eligibility for this service." } }) }} ```

row.cell: As part of our commitment to...

checklist.entry: Write numbers 1 to 9 as words, 10+ as digits
Use "to" not dashes for ranges ("9am to 5pm")

template_block.name: indentation-and-formatting
template_block.title: Indentation and formatting
template_block.statement: Do not use tabs. The Prototype Kit and govuk-frontend use spaces throughout.

template_block.name: nunjucks-formatting
template_block.title: Nunjucks formatting
template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Multi-line macro with aligned properties #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Full name", classes: "govuk-label--l", isPageHeading: true }, id: "full-name", name: "fullName", value: data['fullName'], errorMessage: errors['fullName'] }) }}
{# ✗ Avoid: Single line for complex macros #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Full name", classes: "govuk-label--l", isPageHeading: true }, id: "full-name", name: "fullName" }) }}
{# ✓ Correct: Simple macros can be single line #} {{ govukButton({ text: "Continue" }) }} {{ govukTag({ text: "Draft", classes: "govuk-tag--grey" }) }} ```

template_block.name: html-structure
template_block.title: HTML structure
template_block.statement:
``html ✓ Correct: Proper nesting and indentation

Page title

Paragraph text that might be quite long and wrap across multiple lines in the source.

``

template_block.name: class-ordering
template_block.title: Class ordering
template_block.statement: Apply classes in this order:
```html ✓ Correct order <input class="govuk-input govuk-input--error govuk-!-width-one-half govuk-!-margin-bottom-4" />
✗ Avoid: Random order <input class="govuk-!-margin-bottom-4 govuk-input govuk-!-width-one-half govuk-input--error" /> ```

template_block.name: javascript-routes-js
template_block.title: JavaScript (routes.js)
template_block.statement: ```javascript // ✓ Correct: Clear routing with consistent style router.post("/v2/contact-method", function (request, response) { const contactMethod = request.session.data["contact-method"];
if (contactMethod === "email") { response.redirect("/v2/email-address"); } else { response.redirect("/v2/phone-number"); } });
// ✓ Use const for variables that don't change const name = request.session.data["full-name"]; ```

template_block.name: form-field-naming
template_block.title: Form field naming
template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Consistent hyphenated names #} {{ govukInput({ name: "full-name", id: "full-name" }) }} {{ govukInput({ name: "email-address", id: "email-address" }) }} {{ govukDateInput({ namePrefix: "date-of-birth" }) }}
{# ✗ Avoid: Inconsistent naming styles #} {{ govukInput({ name: "fullName", id: "full-name" }) }} {{ govukInput({ name: "email_address", id: "emailAddress" }) }} ```

template_block.name: comments
template_block.title: Comments
template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# Nunjucks comments for template logic #} {# This section only shows if user selected 'yes' #} {% if data['has-partner'] === 'yes' %} ... {% endif %}
HTML comments for structural notes (visible in source) Check answers section ```
```javascript // JavaScript: Use // for single lines // Redirect based on user's answer

checklist.entry: Use block comments for complex logic explanations
Keep templates simple – complex logic belongs in routes.js, not templates
One question per page – don't combine multiple questions
Match existing patterns – look at similar pages in the codebase first
Don't repeat yourself – use includes for repeated content
Test with real content – avoid lorem ipsum, use realistic data
Preserve data references – keep data['field-name'] access consistent

template_block.name: accessibility-guidelines
template_block.title: Accessibility guidelines
template_block.statement: GOV.UK components are WCAG 2.2 AA compliant by default, but you must use them correctly. These guidelines are mandatory.

template_block.name: labels-and-inputs
template_block.title: Labels and inputs
template_block.statement: Every form input must have an associated label. Never use placeholder text as a substitute for labels.
```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Label associated via 'for' attribute (handled by macro) #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Email address" }, id: "email", name: "email" }) }}
{# ✗ Never: Input without label #} <input class="govuk-input" type="text" name="email">
{# ✗ Never: Placeholder as label #} <input class="govuk-input" type="text" placeholder="Enter email"> ```

template_block.name: hint-texts
template_block.title: Hint texts
template_block.statement: Use hint for help text. It's automatically linked to the input via aria-describedby.

``nunjucks {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "National Insurance number" }, hint: { text: "It's on your National Insurance card. For example, 'QQ 12 34 56 C'." }, id: "ni-number", name: "ni-number" }) }} ``

template_block.name: fieldsets-and-legends
template_block.title: Fieldsets and legends
template_block.statement: Group related inputs (radios, checkboxes, date inputs) with a fieldset. The legend describes the group.

```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Fieldset with legend for grouped inputs #} {{ govukRadios({ name: "contact", fieldset: { legend: { text: "How would you prefer to be contacted?", isPageHeading: true, classes: "govuk-fieldset__legend--l" } }, items: [...] }) }}
{# ✗ Avoid: Radios without fieldset/legend #} <label>Email</label> <input type="radio" name="contact" value="email"> <label>Phone</label> <input type="radio" name="contact" value="phone"> ```

template_block.name: link-text
template_block.title: Link text
template_block.statement: Link text must describe the destination. Avoid generic text like "click here" or "read more".
```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Descriptive link text #} Apply for a driving licence
{# ✗ Avoid: Generic link text #} Click here To apply for a driving licence, read more. ```
When context makes the destination clear visually but not for screen readers, use visually hidden text:

```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Hidden text for screen reader context #} {{ govukSummaryList({ rows: [{ key: { text: "Name" }, value: { text: "Sarah Smith" }, actions: { items: [{ href: "/change-name", text: "Change", visuallyHiddenText: "name" }] } }] }) }}
{# Renders as: "Change name" #} {# Screen reader announces: "Change name" #}
{# For manual implementation: #} Change name ```

template_block.name: heading-hierarchy
template_block.title: Heading hierarchy
template_block.statement: Use headings in order. Don't skip levels. Every page needs one <h1>.

```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Sequential heading levels #}

Apply for a licence

Before you start

...

What you'll need

Documents

{# ✗ Avoid: Skipping levels or multiple h1s #}

Apply for a licence

Before you start

{# Skipped h2 #}

Another section

{# Multiple h1s #} ```
Use isPageHeading: true to make a legend or label the page's <h1>:
``nunjucks {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "What is your email address?", classes: "govuk-label--l", isPageHeading: true }, id: "email", name: "email" }) }} ``

template_block.name: error-message
template_block.title: Error message
template_block.statement: Errors must be:

checklist.entry: Linked to the input via aria-describedby (macros handle this)
Summarised at the top of the page with links to each field
Prefixed with visually hidden "Error:" text (macros handle this)

template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {# Error summary at page top - links to each field #} {{ govukErrorSummary({ titleText: "There is a problem", errorList: [ { text: "Enter your full name", href: "#full-name" }, { text: "Enter a valid email address", href: "#email" } ] }) }}
{# Individual field errors #} {{ govukInput({ label: { text: "Full name" }, id: "full-name", name: "full-name", errorMessage: { text: "Enter your full name" } }) }} ```

template_block.name: focus-states
template_block.title: Focus states
template_block.statement: Never remove or override focus styles. GOV.UK uses a yellow focus ring for visibility.
```css / ✗ Never do this / *:focus { outline: none; }
.govuk-link:focus { outline: none; } ```

template_block.name: images
template_block.title: Images
template_block.statement: All images need appropriate alt text. Decorative images should have empty alt (alt="").
```nunjucks {# Informative image: describe the content #}
{# Decorative image: empty alt #}
{# Complex images: use longer description #} ```

template_block.name: tables
template_block.title: Tables
template_block.statement: Tables must have headers that describe columns/rows.

``nunjucks {{ govukTable({ caption: "Monthly application statistics", captionClasses: "govuk-table__caption--m", firstCellIsHeader: true, head: [ { text: "Month" }, { text: "Applications" }, { text: "Approved" } ], rows: [ [{ text: "January" }, { text: "245" }, { text: "230" }], [{ text: "February" }, { text: "312" }, { text: "298" }] ] }) }} ``

template_block.name: buttons-vs-links
template_block.title: Buttons vs links
template_block.statement: Use the correct element for the action:
```nunjucks {# ✓ Correct: Button for form submission #} {{ govukButton({ text: "Submit application" }) }}
{# ✓ Correct: Link for navigation #} Return to dashboard
{# ✗ Avoid: Link styled as button for actions #} Submit
{# Exception: Start button is a link (navigates to service) #} {{ govukButton({ text: "Start now", href: "/first-question", isStartButton: true }) }} ```

template_block.name: language
template_block.title: Language
template_block.statement: If content is in a different language, mark it:

``html

The French term is carte d'identité.

``

template_block.name: accessibility-checklist
template_block.title: Accessibility checklist
template_block.statement: Before considering a page complete:

checklist.entry: [ ] Headings in sequential order (no skipped levels)
[ ] All form inputs have visible labels
[ ] All form inputs have associated <label> elements
[ ] Error summary present and links to fields
[ ] Visually hidden text added where context needed
[ ] Buttons used for actions, links for navigation

template_block.name: use-prototype-kit-templates
template_block.title: Use Prototype Kit templates
template_block.statement: Templates are available to copy from node_modules/@govuk-prototype-kit/common-templates/templates:

template_block.name: basic-page-structure
template_block.title: Basic page structure
template_block.statement: ```nunjucks {% extends "layouts/main.html" %}
{% set pageName = "What is your name?" %}
{% block beforeContent %} {{ govukBackLink({ text: "Back", href: "javascript:history.back()" }) }} {% endblock %}

{% block content %}
Content here
{% endblock %} ```

template_block.name: linking-to-pages-not-yet-built
template_block.title: Linking to pages not yet built
template_block.statement: When a prototype includes links to pages that haven't been created yet (e.g. header navigation, footer links, or features outside the current testing scope), link them to a shared placeholder page at /not-yet-built.
Create app/views/not-yet-built.html:
```nunjucks {% extends "layouts/main.html" %}
{% set pageName = "Page not built yet" %}
{% block beforeContent %} {{ govukBackLink({ text: "Back", href: "javascript:history.back()" }) }} {% endblock %}

{% block content %}

This page has not been built yet
This part of the prototype is not ready for testing.

Go back to the previous page

{% endblock %} ```
Then link to it from any placeholder navigation:
``nunjucks Account settings ``

template_block.name: design-system-reference
template_block.title: Design System reference
template_block.statement: For component syntax, CSS classes, spacing, typography, and colours, always refer to the live documentation:
Do not hardcode class names or hex values from memory – check the current documentation to ensure accuracy.

template_block.name: basic-route-routes-js
template_block.title: Basic route (routes.js)
template_block.statement: ``javascript router.post("/v2/question-answer", function (request, response) { response.redirect("/v2/next-page"); }); ``

template_block.name: branching-logic
template_block.title: Branching logic
template_block.statement: ```javascript router.post("/v2/contact-preference", function (request, response) { const contact = request.session.data["contact"];
if (contact === "email") { response.redirect("/v2/email-address"); } else if (contact === "phone") { response.redirect("/v2/phone-number"); } else { response.redirect("/v2/postal-address"); } }); ```

template_block.name: accessing-data-in-templates
template_block.title: Accessing data in templates
template_block.statement:
```nunjucks {# Display stored answer #}

{{ data['fullName'] }}

{# With default value #}

{{ data['fullName'] or 'Not provided' }}
{# Pre-fill form field #} {{ govukInput({ value: data['fullName'] }) }} ```

template_block.name: setting-variable-within-a-view
template_block.title: Setting variable within a view
template_block.statement: ``nunjucks {% set fullName = "Joe Blogs" %} ``
